page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.c6r.large is an ARM-based compute-optimized virtual machine instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features 2 dedicated physical cores on an Ampere Altra processor running at 2.8 GHz, paired with 4.0 GB of system memory. This configuration yields a 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, allocating 2.0 GB of memory per core. The instance does not include local storage or GPU hardware, relying instead on network-attached storage and featuring a baseline network bandwidth of 1 Gbps.

Performance benchmarks indicate that the dedicated Ampere Altra cores deliver average single-core CPU performance, as demonstrated by a stress-ng single-core score of 1824.28. However, multi-core and memory subsystem performance are more constrained. The multi-core stress-ng score of 3617.67 and the PassMark CPU score of 1594.77 fall into lower performance tiers. Memory bandwidth is also limited, with uncached large-block read and write speeds of 26.08 GB/sec and 16.11 GB/sec respectively, and a PassMark memory score of 1056.07.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the ecs.c6r.large offers a low-cost entry point into dedicated ARM64 architecture without the resource-sharing overhead of burstable instances. The tradeoff is lower overall memory bandwidth and modest computational throughput. This hardware profile is best suited for lightweight, single-threaded or moderately threaded workloads such as static web serving, basic Redis key-value operations, and simple utility scripting that do not require high memory capacity or intensive vector processing.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.c6r.large is an entry-level, compute-optimized virtual machine utilizing the KVM hypervisor and ARM64 architecture. Powered by a 2.8 GHz Ampere Altra processor, it provides 2 dedicated physical cores and 4.0 GB of system memory, establishing a 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio. The instance lacks local storage and GPU accelerators, relying on a 1 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Benchmark data shows average single-core CPU performance, though multi-core execution and memory bandwidth remain relatively weak, with uncached read speeds of 26.08 GB/sec. Qualitatively, this instance offers a cost-effective option for dedicated ARM resources, trading off high-end throughput for predictable, non-shared CPU allocation. It is best suited for lightweight workloads such as static web serving, basic Redis caching, and simple single-threaded applications.
og_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.c6r.large is a KVM-based ARM64 instance featuring 2 dedicated Ampere Altra cores at 2.8 GHz and 4.0 GB of RAM, designed for lightweight dedicated compute workloads.
meta_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.c6r.large is an ARM64 virtual server with 2 dedicated Ampere Altra cores, 4.0 GB RAM, and 1 Gbps baseline network bandwidth.
tagline: A dedicated ARM64 compute instance offering predictable single-core performance and a balanced entry-level resource profile for lightweight workloads.
bullet_points: 2 dedicated Ampere Altra ARM64 physical cores running at 2.8 GHz
4.0 GB of system memory providing a 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio
1 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with no local storage or GPU
Average single-core performance suitable for lightweight single-threaded tasks
Predictable dedicated CPU allocation without burstable resource sharing
Best suited for static web serving, basic Redis caching, and utility scripts
